scope: DM1 V1 movement core lane: input -> command -> turning/collision -> timing/sensors

redmcsbCitations.claim: mouse movement rows map visible arrows and dungeon viewport to command ids
PC-34 keyboard movement rows map keypad/arrow codes to turn and step commands
PC input normalizes shifted extended movement keys before command-table lookup
F0380 locks the queue, keeps gated movement queued, dequeues one command, then dispatches turns and steps
F0365 turning sets stop-wait, handles stairs as a special case, otherwise applies direction change with sensor boundaries

F0366 steps charge stamina before resolution, blocks walls/doors/fakewalls/groups before F0267 for non-empty parties, preserves BUG0_85 by skipping group collision when the party is empty, and applies cooldown only after accepted movement
relative forward/right deltas are applied from direction tables before current-map square lookup
movement cadence is derived from load, feet wounds, and Boots of Speed
F0267 owns the source/destination move-result contract and projectile-impact precheck
accepted party moves record result/timing/scent then run source leave and destination enter sensor passes

firestaffEvidence.claim: PC-34 input rows, queue lock, gated movement retention, pending-click replay, and blocked-input discard
F0380-to-F0365/F0366 seam: turns bypass movement gates, steps apply stamina/collision/group blocking/timing
pure movement legality and target-square result semantics behind F0366, including the empty-party group-collision bug
post-step cadence mirrors F0310/F0366 timing side effects
behavior coverage for queued input through turn/step dispatch, collision, stamina, timing, and sensor order
integration coverage for PC-34 movement queue, collision, turn, sensor, timing edges, and BUG0_85 empty-party group passage
